title: Hawkes-lite pit-wave intensity features
summary: Public F1 strategy analyses and recent game-theoretic pit-stop work both emphasize reactive stops: teams cover undercuts, exploit traffic windows, and trigger clustered pit waves.
rationale: Public F1 strategy analyses and recent game-theoretic pit-stop work both emphasize reactive stops: teams cover undercuts, exploit traffic windows, and trigger clustered pit waves. A self-exciting event view is missing from the current baseline, which has rival features but not explicit multi-lap field intensity.
implementation_hint: Create forward-only race-level and local-neighborhood intensities from prior pit events: decayed counts over the previous 1-3 laps, prior pits among nearby positions, and prior pits among same-compound or same-strategy peers. Interact these with own cooldown, tyre age, and laps-remaining features. Keep all aggregates strictly prior-lap only.
expected_effect: Should help around ambiguous clustered windows where many drivers stop in reaction to one another rather than solely because of their own tyre state.
risk: Very easy to leak if current-lap or future rows enter the aggregates. In sparse-stop races the signal may be noisy and require shrinkage or simple EMA features instead of a full point-process fit.
